noveltyJustification: New D2 mapping: kasper's stuck-watchdog maps to an opt-in active-slot abort/requeue path, not a literal latch clear, and reuses our existing timeout retry ledger.

title: Promote lag ceiling from warning-only to opt-in abort/requeue recovery
summary: Kasper clears a hung evaluation latch after its max duration; our analogous lag ceiling currently emits only a warning.

title: Add active-slot registry before freeing a hung pool slot
summary: Our pool tracks active as a count, so recovery needs an active item registry and abort hook before it can safely settle a hung lineage.

title: Route forced stall abort through existing timeout retry ledger
summary: A stall abort should synthesize a timeout-like rejected result and reuse retry_scheduled/maxRetries rather than add another stuck state.

title: Test forced recovery without reviving backpressure false positives
summary: Tests should assert one forced abort/requeue for a hung slot and silence for normal width greater than concurrency backpressure.

title: Keep lag ceiling as pool progress watchdog, not second global timeout
summary: fanout-run already kills children on lineage timeout; lag ceiling should target no-progress-while-queued earlier recovery.

Copying kasper literally would be unsafe here: clearing pool accounting without killing or settling the child could oversubscribe concurrency and leave the old child writing into its lineage directory.
